insight.headline: Storefront API update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel.
insight.developer_impact: Developers using the Storefront API should update their queries to include the channelId argument when necessary. This change provides more flexibility in querying data and can be useful for merchants with multiple sales channels. Developers should review their existing queries and update them to take advantage of this new feature. The Storefront API documentation should be consulted for more information on using the @inContext directive with channelId.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants with multiple sales channels may see more accurate and relevant data in their storefronts, as developers can now query data specific to each channel.
insight.why_it_matters: This update provides more flexibility and accuracy in querying data, which can lead to a better merchant and customer experience. It also demonstrates Shopify's commitment to improving the Storefront API and providing more features for developers.

also_worth_knowing.text: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, allowing developers to build and test against new inventory APIs.
CLI version 4.5.2 fixes a warning when running theme commands in valid theme directories and bumps Shopify/theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: App Store requirements have been updated to include using a unique name for apps to reduce merchant confusion.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API @inContext directive now supports an optional channelId argument, allowing for channel-specific context in queries.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type now includes productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components, allowing apps to access component-level discount allocations.
CLI version 4.5.0 introduces new store commands, including listing and store creation.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1
updates.summary: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1 reads disabled command metadata and preserves actionable build errors during h2 deploy.

updates.title: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38
updates.summary: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38 adds an explicit ~ app alias to new Hydrogen projects and uses jsconfig.json for JavaScript projects.

recap.headline: Shopify updates focus on GraphQL Admin API, Hydrogen, and App Store requirements

themes.title: GraphQL Admin API updates
themes.detail: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, and the card-deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate. Additionally, the Storefront API `@inContext` directive now supports `channelId` and new fields have been added to `ExchangeLineItem`.

themes.title: Hydrogen and CLI updates
themes.detail: New versions of `@shopify/cli-hydrogen` and `@shopify/create-hydrogen` have been released with patch changes, including non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and improved error handling. The CLI has also been updated with new store commands and bumped versions of `cli-hydrogen` and `theme-tools` packages.

themes.title: App Store requirements and POS UI Extensions
themes.detail: App Store requirement 4.1.2 has been added, requiring unique app names to reduce merchant confusion. POS UI Extensions API version 2026-07 now includes discount allocation details for product bundle components in cart line item data.

actions.text: Enable physical inventory feature preview on a development store
Update apps to use the new `@inContext` directive with `channelId`
Review and update app names to comply with App Store requirement 4.1.2
